verbGlossary.ACCEPTED BY: {source} formally acknowledges that a milestone or deliverable meets contractual requirements, transferring responsibility or initiating the next phase.
verbGlossary.ADMINISTERED BY: {source} manages the contractual lifecycle of a procurement instrument, handling amendments, compliance tracking, and documentation.
verbGlossary.ANALYZED BY: {source} examines bid data or contract metrics to identify cost drivers, outliers, and competitive positioning across submissions.
verbGlossary.ASSEMBLED BY: {source} compiles multiple documents, drawings, specifications, and exhibits into a complete package for issuance or closeout.
verbGlossary.ASSESSED BY: {source} determines the financial exposure or impact of a contractual clause, calculating damages or penalties based on project performance.
verbGlossary.AUDITED BY: {source} performs compliance verification against regulatory or contractual requirements, checking records for accuracy and completeness.
verbGlossary.AUTHORIZED BY: {source} grants executive-level approval for {target} award or procurement action, committing the company to a binding obligation.
verbGlossary.AWARDED BY: {source} selects and formally designates a contractor or vendor as the recipient of {target} based on qualifications, price, and evaluation criteria.
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED BY: {source} compares a procurement metric against historical portfolio data or industry standards to evaluate procurement effectiveness.
verbGlossary.CALCULATED BY: {source} computes monetary values tied to schedule performance, applying contractual formulas to determine delay-related costs.
verbGlossary.CERTIFIED BY: {source} attests through formal inspection that a project milestone has been physically achieved and meets specification requirements.
verbGlossary.CLOSED BY: {source} completes all remaining contractual obligations, resolves open items, and formally terminates {target}.
verbGlossary.COMMISSIONED BY: {source} verifies that installed systems operate per design intent and contract specifications through functional performance testing.
verbGlossary.COMPARED BY: {source} evaluates competing proposals side by side against scope requirements, pricing, schedule, and qualifications to inform award decisions.
verbGlossary.COMPILED BY: {source} gathers and organizes all required project records, warranties, and as-built documents into a final deliverable package.
verbGlossary.COORDINATED BY: {source} synchronizes the timing and logistics of a procurement action across multiple stake{target}s and project teams.
verbGlossary.DEFINED BY: {target} establishes the technical scope, deliverables, and performance criteria that {source} instrument must satisfy.
verbGlossary.DISTRIBUTED BY: {source} sends bid packages to qualified bidders, managing the solicitation timeline and ensuring equitable access to project information.
verbGlossary.DRAFTED BY: {source} prepares the initial version of a procurement document, assembling requirements, terms, and evaluation criteria for review.
verbGlossary.DRAWN BY: {source} authorizes expenditure against a contractual allowance for work that falls within the defined allowance scope.
verbGlossary.ENFORCED BY: {source} ensures compliance with contractual terms in the field or through administrative action, holding parties accountable to obligations.
verbGlossary.EVALUATED BY: {source} scores and ranks submissions against defined criteria, producing a recommendation for contract award or vendor selection.
verbGlossary.EXECUTED BY: {source} signs and formalizes {target} instrument, creating a legally binding agreement between parties.
verbGlossary.EXPEDITED BY: {source} actively follows up with vendors and suppliers to accelerate delivery of materials or equipment against purchase order timelines.
verbGlossary.FILED BY: {source} submits bond documentation to the appropriate parties, ensuring payment protection is in place before work begins.
verbGlossary.FORECASTED BY: {source} projects future change order exposure or contract growth based on current trends and remaining project scope.
verbGlossary.INITIATED BY: {source} starts a procurement process by identifying the need for goods, services, or trade contractors on a project.
verbGlossary.INTERPRETED BY: {source} provides guidance on the meaning and application of contract terms when disputes or ambiguities arise during execution.
verbGlossary.LEVELED BY: {source} normalizes bid submissions to a common scope basis, adjusting for exclusions, qualifications, and alternates to enable fair comparison.
verbGlossary.MAINTAINED BY: {source} keeps a long-term contract current by managing renewals, amendments, and rate adjustments across multiple project assignments.
verbGlossary.MANAGED BY: {source} oversees the full lifecycle of {target} or procurement instrument from initiation through closeout, including status tracking.
verbGlossary.MEASURED BY: {source} quantifies a procurement metric by collecting and aggregating data across bid events and contract awards.
verbGlossary.MONITORED BY: {source} tracks {target} term or allowance balance over time, reviewing trends and flagging when thresholds are approached.
verbGlossary.NEGOTIATED BY: {source} conducts commercial negotiations on pricing, scope, and contract terms with owners, subcontractors, or vendors.
verbGlossary.PLACED BY: {source} issues a purchase order to a vendor, specifying quantities, delivery dates, and pricing for materials or equipment.
verbGlossary.PROCURED BY: {source} obtains insurance coverage or risk-transfer instruments required by the project, selecting carriers and negotiating premiums.
verbGlossary.RATIFIED BY: {source} gives final executive endorsement to a master agreement, confirming it as the governing framework for future project work.
verbGlossary.REPORTED BY: {source} compiles and submits diversity participation data and DBE certification status to meet contractual or regulatory requirements.
verbGlossary.REQUIRED BY: {source} specifies that a bond or surety instrument must be provided as a condition of bid submission or contract execution.
verbGlossary.REVIEWED BY: {source} examines {target} document for technical accuracy, scope alignment, and completeness before it advances in the workflow.
verbGlossary.SCREENED BY: {source} evaluates qualification submissions against minimum criteria, shortlisting respondents who meet threshold requirements.
verbGlossary.SECURED BY: {source} obtains a performance bond or surety from the awarded contractor, protecting the project against non-performance.
verbGlossary.STIPULATED BY: {source} specifies retainage percentages and release conditions within {target}, establishing the withholding framework.
verbGlossary.TRACKED BY: {source} monitors a financial or schedule metric over time, maintaining records and reporting status against contractual requirements.
verbGlossary.VALIDATED BY: {source} confirms that an insurance certificate meets project-specific coverage requirements, endorsements, and policy limits.
